notes:
## Review Summary (2026-06-15)

**36 findings filed, 3 duplicates closed → 33 unique findings**

| Priority | Count | Label |
|----------|-------|-------|
| P0       | 3     | Must fix |
| P1       | 12    | Should fix |
| P2       | 15    | Worth fixing |
| P3       | 3     | Polish |

### Confidence Calibration

Review confidence: 25% chance of missed bug.

Least confident areas:
- hashicorp-vault (excluded from edge-case review per standing policy — not integration-tested yet)
- bitwarden (OnceCell refresh finding needs live validation)
- wolfhsm (stub-only backend, hard to assess real behavior)
- secretx-core JSON scanner (complex byte-level parsing, only partial coverage of edge cases)

Would raise confidence if:
- Integration tests against real Vault, Bitwarden, and WolfHSM servers
- Fuzz testing of the JSON scanner (skip_string_b, json_skip_string)
- Cross-platform CI (macOS/Windows) to validate keyring cfg-gating

### Notable positives (from review-crypto agent)
- SecretValue correctly avoids Debug/Display/Clone/Serialize
- File backend atomic write (O_CREAT_NEW + fsync + rename) is correct
- Cache uses Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> and never holds lock across .await
- All URL/path inputs validated at construction
- No deprecated crypto primitives in use
- All RNG usage delegated to RustCrypto (implicit OsRng)
title: Review: crate-secretsx 2026-06-15
